finding_results.notes: The revision now names coercive control explicitly in the school and court sequences, and ties it to concrete consequences: institutional escalation risk (welfare report/counselor/AP/ACS), social fallout with Zoe, and body-level punishment observed by peers.

finding_results.notes: School-day interruptions now escalate by distinct leverage shifts rather than repeating compliance reminders: office-call threat, concealment script, counselor contradiction risk, explicit training punishment, coordinated lie for follow-up, real-time gate proof demand, then immediate punishment consequence.
Scene narration was converted to past tense throughout the chapter while preserving present-tense dialogue and text artifacts.

summary: Chapter 2 was revised to past-tense narration and structurally tightened around coercive-control causality: text interruptions now produce non-redundant leverage shifts, institutional/social/physical consequences are explicit, and the locker-to-court escalation lands as a single causal chain.
